assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Each criterion file (criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md) individually marks its verdict as PASS: (1) single license filter via is_in with split+validate, (2) comma-separated via split(',') producing multiple identifiers and Condition::any() with is_in, (3) 400 via Expression::parse returning AppError::BadRequest, (4) pagination via filter-before-paginate ordering with total from filtered query, (5) unchanged response shape with PaginatedResults<PackageSummary> return type preserved.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table: 'Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 changed files match the task specification exactly'. Intent Alignment section provides file-by-file comparison table with three rows: 'modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs (modify) | Modified | MATCH', 'modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs (modify) | Modified | MATCH', 'tests/api/package.rs (create) | Created | MATCH'. States: 'No files outside the task scope were modified.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created'. No output files indicate sub-task creation.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files: report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. These are verification/analysis documents. No source code files (e.g., .rs, .toml) appear in the outputs. The report references code via quoted snippets but does not indicate any file modifications were made.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and states 'All acceptance criteria are satisfied by the PR diff. The implementation follows existing patterns in the codebase, scope is contained to the specified files, and comprehensive tests are provided.' This is a presentation of findings. No merge command, merge action, or auto-merge indication appears anywhere in the output files.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No passwords, API keys, secrets, or private keys detected'. Security section details: 'A line-by-line review of the diff found no instances of: Hardcoded passwords or credentials, API keys or tokens, Private keys or certificates, Connection strings with embedded credentials, Environment variable references to secrets.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | New test file `tests/api/package.rs` created with 4 integration tests'. Style/Conventions section: 'Test Change Classification: ADDITIVE. The PR creates a new test file (`tests/api/package.rs`) with 4 new integration tests. No existing tests were modified or removed.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table contains all specified verdict rows: Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), Commit Traceability (PASS) under Intent Alignment section; Sensitive Patterns (PASS) under Security section; CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS), Verification Commands (N/A) under Correctness section; Test Quality (PASS, with 'Eval Quality: N/A' noted) and Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE) under Style/Conventions section. The report has explicit section headers for all four domains: 'Intent Alignment', 'Security', 'Correctness', 'Style/Conventions'.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment: file-by-file scope comparison table with 3 rows mapping Task Specification to PR Diff with detailed descriptions of changes per file. Security: enumerated list of 5 specific pattern categories scanned (passwords, API keys, private keys, connection strings, env vars) plus analysis of validate_license_param and SeaORM parameterized queries. Correctness: 5 numbered acceptance criteria with code snippets from list.rs and service/mod.rs, explaining logic flow and citing specific test names. Style/Conventions: test quality assessment referencing test patterns (#[test_context], #[tokio::test]), Given/When/Then structure, assert_eq! conventions, and 4-test-to-4-requirement mapping.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table: 'Test Quality | PASS | Eval Quality: N/A'. The Test Quality verdict is PASS (not affected by Eval Quality), and Eval Quality is explicitly listed as N/A.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md shows Eval Quality as N/A (in Test Quality row). Root-Cause Investigation is 'N/A | No sub-tasks created'. No output files reference eval failure sub-tasks or sub-task creation of any kind.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table rows: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created'. These appear as top-level rows in the summary table (lines 5-6), before the domain-specific rows (Scope Containment through Test Change Classification, lines 7-15), and are not nested under any domain section (Intent Alignment, Security, Correctness, Style/Conventions).
